prevalence.trend: stable; HSV-1 genital infections increasing in younger adults

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; recurrent morbidity and psychosocial impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical lesions with PCR/NAAT confirmation; type-specific serology for unclear cases

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions (HSV-1 and HSV-2 combined)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with access to antivirals and counseling

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: PCR widely available; clinical diagnosis common in primary care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acyclovir/valacyclovir episodic or suppressive therapy; counseling and partner notification as appropriate
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and under-recognition of asymptomatic infection
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad access to diagnostics and antivirals across EU health systems
